_comment: Heuristic grep-set for grm-fleet-audit's capability-overlap checklist item (#412, v3.97; pairs with the rule-of-two extraction policy, #411). One entry per component-taxonomy capability (docs/grimoire/design/component-taxonomy.md §3 is the single source of truth for the key set -- capability_overlap.py surfaces, never silently drops, a key here that the taxonomy doc no longer lists). Each capability maps to a 'patterns' array of Python `re` regex strings (case-sensitive, matched per line) that heuristically signal a HAND-ROLLED implementation of that capability -- code built directly from a raw ecosystem primitive inside a repo's own source, rather than consumed from a shared internal component via vendor.toml/grm-sync-deps. A repo counts as ONE hand-rolled implementation of a capability if ANY pattern matches ANY line in ANY of its files (repo-level dedup, not a match-count) -- see capability_overlap.py's CapabilityOverlapScanner.scan(). This is a heuristic surface for human triage, not a certainty signal: a hit warrants a look (and, at >=2 repos, an extraction-ticket draft), never an automatic file-and-forget. To add a capability: add one key matching a component-taxonomy §3 term with a non-empty 'patterns' array. To add a pattern to an existing capability: append a regex string; prefer a construct that signals 'this repo built the subsystem' (a middleware class, a connection constructor, a route-registration call, a repository/store class) over a bare common-utility call (e.g. avoid something as generic as a raw `os.environ.get(` line) to keep the false-positive rate low.
